title: Intro
en: Welcome. This tutorial covers the daily RP Rental workflow now migrated from manual steps into Odoo.

title: What Changed
en: Odoo is the source of truth for customers, equipment, availability, contracts, deliveries, returns, quality checks, and billing.

title: Golden Rule
en: If it is not in Odoo, it did not happen. Keep every step updated as you work.

title: Navigation Basics
en: Start from the main Apps menu. Use the global search bar to quickly find Customers, Rentals, and Products.

title: Roles and Access
en: Your role controls what you can do. If a button is missing, request access instead of working around the process.

title: Customers
en: Before creating a quote, confirm the customer exists and the billing and delivery contacts are correct.

title: Create or Update a Customer
en: Create new customers only when needed. Add phone, email, addresses, and set any payment terms required by the business.

title: Products and Assets
en: Rental products represent what you sell. Rental assets represent the physical units that get delivered and returned.

title: Finding Equipment Fast
en: Use the imported catalog and product codes to search quickly. Confirm the item is marked as rentable and available.

title: Availability Check
en: Always confirm availability for the requested dates before promising equipment to the customer.

title: Create a Rental Quote
en: Create a new quotation for the customer, then add the rental products and set the rental start and end dates.

title: Rental Dates First
en: Set the rental period before validating pricing. Dates drive availability and the correct rental price calculation.

title: Pricing and Rate Logic
en: Pricing follows the imported rate matrix. Confirm daily, weekly, and monthly cycles align with the agreed commercial terms.

title: Confirm the Contract
en: When the customer approves, confirm the quotation. This becomes the rental contract and triggers the operational workflow.

title: Delivery Preparation
en: Review the delivery order, ensure the correct warehouse and destination, and prepare assets for dispatch.

title: Validate Delivery
en: Validate the delivery in Odoo when the equipment leaves. Record any required evidence and confirm the on-site location.

title: Extensions and Changes
en: If dates or quantities change, update the contract immediately. Do not track changes only in chat or email.

title: Exchange Contracts
en: If an asset is replaced, record the exchange so traceability and billing remain correct.

title: Partial Returns
en: For partial returns, process the return picking and adjust the contract lines so invoicing stays accurate.

title: Full Return
en: When all equipment returns, validate the return picking. This is the handoff into quality control.

title: Return Quality Check
en: Run the Rental Return inspection. Capture condition notes and issues so maintenance and billing decisions are supported.

title: Ready-To-Rent Check
en: Before sending equipment out again, complete the Ready-To-Rent inspection. This prevents avoidable failures in the field.

title: Billing Cycle Overview
en: Billing is driven by the contract and time. Generate invoices based on the agreed billing cadence.

title: 3-3-3 Rule (If Applicable)
en: If your process uses the 3-3-3 rule, apply it consistently: daily, weekly, and monthly billing logic must match the contract.

title: Create and Send Invoices
en: Create the invoice from the rental order, review line items and taxes, then send it to the customer from Odoo.

title: Payments and Reconciliation
en: When payment is received, register it and reconcile. Keep Accounting accurate so reporting stays reliable.

title: Operational Reporting
en: Use reports to monitor open rentals, asset utilization, overdue returns, invoices due, and quality backlog.

title: Closeout
en: Once the rental is fully returned, quality is completed, and invoicing is done, close the loop and archive evidence.

title: End
en: You are ready to run the daily workflow in Odoo using your real catalog, assets, and rates.
